hero.title: Self Recyclable Trash Can
hero.description: An intelligent prototype designed for automatic identification and sorting of recyclables using low-latency sensors and Arduino logic.

overview.title: Problem & Tech Solution
overview.p1: Solid waste production is constantly growing. Despite awareness campaigns, the difficulty of proper sorting results in the waste of recyclable materials and increases environmental impact.
overview.p2: The core challenge was to replace complex computer vision with an efficient, cost-effective hardware solution. This system utilizes light-frequency analysis to distinguish materials, making the process practical and preventing common disposal errors.
overview.p3: By focusing on color-coded waste streams, we achieved a response time of less than 500ms for material categorization.

objectives.title: Core Objectives
objectives.obj1: Automatically separate metal, paper, and plastic in real-time.
objectives.obj2: Propose a low-cost, modular hardware solution for domestic or community use.
objectives.obj3: Track and count materials (Serial Logging) for integration with public incentive systems.

steps.step1_desc: Initially planned with AI via camera, the project pivoted to TCS230 Color Sensors to ensure technical feasibility and near-instant response times.
steps.step2_desc: Development of a gravity-assisted inclined ramp. This design ensures consistent material positioning for accurate readings.
steps.step3_desc: The system maps RGB values. Arduino then triggers strong Dual-Servos to redirect the waste to the correct bin with zero manual input.

items.software_desc: Implementation of a real-time system that records every item processed (metals, plastics), allowing precise monitoring of recycling efficiency via Arduino serial/data logs.
items.social_desc: A gamified proposal where neighborhoods reaching recycling goals trigger social rewards, such as wheelchair donations or community cultural events supported by local partners.
items.java_desc: Developing a robust desktop dashboard in C++ to monitor city-wide waste distribution and sensor health.
items.mobile_desc: Integration with a dedicated Android app for citizens to track their "Environmental Score" via Bluetooth/WiFi.
items.solar_desc: Implementation of solar panels to ensure 100% sustainable operation in public squares and schools.
